rows.threat_model_match: trusted controller + untrusted accelerator; proxy-evaluated, not formal
rows.mathematical_reason: right-mask identity preserves token-axis concatenation and operator-compatible nonlinear islands
rows.safe_paper_wording: Under our proxy attackers and in our tested configurations the full bundle keeps the worst-case classifier near random chance.

rows.threat_model_match: trusted controller + untrusted accelerator; proxy-evaluated, not formal
rows.mathematical_reason: right-mask identity preserves token-axis concatenation and operator-compatible nonlinear islands
rows.safe_paper_wording: Same as the default full bundle but with the experimental opt-in inter_block_masked_boundary mode; reported as proxy-supported, not formal.

rows.paper_name: Slalom: Fast, Verifiable and Private Execution of Neural Networks in Trusted Hardware (ICLR 2019)
rows.threat_model_match: Slalom: trusted CPU + untrusted accelerator with Freivalds verification
rows.unsupported_reason: generation / KV append / LoRA training out of paper scope
rows.mathematical_reason: Slalom defines a delegated linear primitive; nonlinear and generation paths are not part of the paper.
rows.safe_paper_wording: We directly implement the Slalom delegated linear primitive and the Freivalds-style verification check.

rows.paper_name: DarKnight: An Accelerated Framework for Privacy and Integrity Preserving Deep Learning (USENIX Security 2021)
rows.threat_model_match: DarKnight: SGX + GPU coding scheme; semi-honest
rows.unsupported_reason: general k>=3 coding, integrity coding, and full SGX pipeline not reproduced
rows.mathematical_reason: k=2 additive sharing recovers y = (x + r)W - rW = xW.
rows.safe_paper_wording: We directly implement the k=2 DarKnight additive-sharing skeleton over a linear layer.

rows.paper_name: Amulet (matrix-obfuscation family); referenced as the static PHQ primitive
rows.unsupported_reason: A generic invertible left mask P_t does not commute with token-axis concatenation unless P_t is block-diagonal aligned with the prefix masks. Refreshing P per step therefore invalidates the existing masked cache and forces re-computation.
rows.mathematical_reason: A generic invertible left mask P_t does not commute with token-axis concatenation unless P_t is block-diagonal aligned with the prefix masks. Refreshing P per step therefore invalidates the existing masked cache and forces re-computation.
rows.safe_paper_wording: We directly implement the Amulet static PHQ identity and report the autoregressive KV-append counterexample.

rows.paper_name: Arrow (nonlinear-primitive obfuscation; specific formula not available in repository materials)
rows.unsupported_reason: Arrow primitive is unavailable in this repository
rows.mathematical_reason: Arrow paper specifies a nonlinear obfuscation primitive whose closed-form formula is not present in the repo's reference materials at the time of this commit.
rows.safe_paper_wording: The Arrow nonlinear primitive is not available in the repository materials; we record it as unavailable rather than substituting a proxy.

rows.kv_append_result: unsupported: polynomial-only network has no KV cache
rows.threat_model_match: CryptoNets: client-side HE; cloud sees ciphertexts (not modelled here)
rows.unsupported_reason: no HE library used; arithmetic skeleton only
rows.mathematical_reason: softmax / RoPE / RMSNorm / SwiGLU are not polynomial under bounded multiplicative depth.
rows.safe_paper_wording: We implement the CryptoNets x^2 polynomial-activation skeleton in plaintext; we do NOT implement the full cryptographic protocol.

rows.unsupported_reason: no executable cryptographic protocol implemented; cost dimensions only
rows.mathematical_reason: rounds=multi-round HE convolution + garbled-circuit ReLU; several rounds per nonlinear layer; modulus_bits=60-180 (BFV; depends on tile and depth); online_offline=online: HE matvec + GC ReLU per layer; offline: key generation + GC table prep
rows.safe_paper_wording: GAZELLE (USENIX Security 2018) is included as a cost-model-only baseline; we do NOT execute its cryptographic protocol and we do NOT produce a measured runtime.

rows.unsupported_reason: no executable cryptographic protocol implemented; cost dimensions only
rows.mathematical_reason: rounds=linear layers via HE; nonlinear approximated by quadratic + secret sharing; multiple rounds; modulus_bits=120-200; online_offline=online dominated by share computation; offline by preprocessing
rows.safe_paper_wording: Delphi (USENIX Security 2020) is included as a cost-model-only baseline; we do NOT execute its cryptographic protocol and we do NOT produce a measured runtime.

rows.unsupported_reason: no executable cryptographic protocol implemented; cost dimensions only
rows.mathematical_reason: rounds=two-party additive sharing; multiple online rounds per layer for multiplication triples; modulus_bits=ring Z_{2^64} (no HE; secret sharing); online_offline=online dominated by share comm.; offline by Beaver triple generation
rows.safe_paper_wording: SecureML (IEEE S&P 2017) is included as a cost-model-only baseline; we do NOT execute its cryptographic protocol and we do NOT produce a measured runtime.

rows.unsupported_reason: no executable cryptographic protocol implemented; cost dimensions only
rows.mathematical_reason: rounds=HE-based linear + GC-based ReLU; multiple rounds per nonlinear layer; modulus_bits=60-180 (BFV); online_offline=online matvec + GC eval; offline keygen + GC prep
rows.safe_paper_wording: MiniONN (CCS 2017) is included as a cost-model-only baseline; we do NOT execute its cryptographic protocol and we do NOT produce a measured runtime.

limitations: Each baseline implements only the primitive(s) listed in its BaselineSelfDeclaration; the original papers' full systems are NOT reproduced.
Cost-model rows (Gazelle / Delphi / SecureML / MiniONN) do not produce measured runtimes; directly_comparable_on_runtime is False.
Threat models differ across baselines; the comparison is *primitive-functional* and *cost-model*, not a claim that all rows defend against the same adversary.
Amulet decoder rows return UnsupportedResult with mathematical_reason; Amulet KV append counterexample is itself the experimental result.
Arrow row is recorded as missing-formula rather than substituted with a generic proxy.
CryptoNets row is an arithmetic skeleton only; no HE library is used; no encryption is performed.
Local CPU emulation only -- not real TEE wall-time and not GPU throughput.
No formal / cryptographic / semantic security is claimed for any row.
Reports publish summary statistics; raw tensors / masks / adapters / gradients are never emitted.
